summary: The test suite covers all core requirements well: CSV parsing, active-employee filtering, per-department averages and headcounts, overall statistics, report generation, and file output. An end-to-end integration test with real fixture files ties the pipeline together, and the all-inactive fixture tests the zero-active edge case. Design is clean: tests are organized by component, factory helpers (MakeEmployees, ActiveEmployees, FixturePath) are DRY, temp files use GUIDs for isolation, and finally-blocks ensure cleanup. TDD intent is clearly signaled via comments. Rigor is the weakest dimension: invalid-date parsing (DateOnly TryParseExact failure path) has no test despite the implementation having that branch; too-many-columns is untested (only too-few); whitespace-only lines and blank-line handling are not verified; case-sensitivity of 'Active' is never probed; and single-employee boundary conditions are absent. The integration assertion Assert.Contains("7", report) is fragile — it can match any '7' in the report, not specifically the active-employee count. Overall the suite is trustworthy for regression detection on the happy path and the covered edges, but a determined mutation could survive by breaking the date-parsing error path or case-sensitivity behavior undetected.
